paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abraham journeyed from thence toward the land of the South, and dwelt between Kadesh and Shur. And he sojourned in Gerar.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abraham said of Sarah his wife, She is my sister. And Abimelech king of Gerar sent, and took Sarah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But God came to Abimelech in a dream of the night, and said to him, Behold, thou art but a dead man, because of the woman whom thou hast taken. For she is a man`s wife.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now Abimelech had not come near her. And he said, Lord, wilt thou slay even a righteous nation?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Said he not himself unto me, She is my sister? And she, even she herself said, He is my brother. In the integrity of my heart and the innocency of my hands have I done this.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And God said unto him in the dream, Yea, I know that in the integrity of thy heart thou has done this, and I also withheld thee from sinning against me. Therefore suffered I thee not to touch her.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now therefore restore the man`s wife. For he is a prophet, and he shall pray for thee, and thou shalt live. And if thou restore her not, know thou that thou shalt surely die, thou, and all that are thine.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants, and told all these things in their ear. And the men were sore afraid.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said unto him, What hast thou done unto us? And wherein have I sinned against thee, that thou hast brought on me and on my kingdom a great sin? Thou hast done deeds unto me that ought not to be done.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abimelech said unto Abraham, What sawest thou, that thou hast done this thing?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abraham said, Because I thought, Surely the fear of God is not in this place. And they will slay me for my wife`s sake.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And moreover she is indeed my sister, the daughter of my father, but not the daughter of my mother; and she became my wife:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and it came to pass, when God caused me to wander from my father`s house, that I said unto her, This is thy kindness which thou shalt show unto me. At every place whither we shall come, say of me, He is my brother.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abimelech took sheep and oxen, and men-servants and women-servants, and gave them unto Abraham, and restored him Sarah his wife.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abimelech said, Behold, my land is before thee. Dwell where it pleaseth thee.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And unto Sarah he said, Behold, I have given thy brother a thousand pieces of silver. Behold, it is for thee a covering of the eyes to all that are with thee. And in respect of all thou art righted.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Abraham prayed unto God. And God healed Abimelech, and his wife, and his maid-servants. And they bare children.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: For Jehovah had fast closed up all the wombs of the house of Abimelech, because of Sarah, Abraham`s wife.
